content.verdict: Amanpuri — meaning 'Place of Peace' in Sanskrit — was the very first Aman resort, opened in 1988 on Pansea Beach in Phuket, and it remains the gold standard of intimate Thai luxury. Thirty-five pavilions and forty private pool villas cascade down a coconut palm-covered hillside to a private white sand beach on the Andaman Sea. The sense of serene, unhurried privacy is unmatched in Southeast Asia — this is a resort where staff remember your name before you arrive, where menus are personalized to your tastes, and where the world's worries genuinely dissolve. For honeymooners seeking the absolute pinnacle of romantic Thailand, Amanpuri is without equal.
content.best_room: The entry-level Pavilions are already extraordinary — Thai-style teak structures with private plunge pools, open-air salas, and king beds draped in white linen with coconut palm views. For ultimate romance, book a one-bedroom Pool Villa directly on the hillside facing the Andaman Sea, where a private infinity pool and butler service create a level of privacy and indulgence that is genuinely transformative.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & First Evening at Pansea Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: A private speedboat or helicopter transfer from Phuket airport deposits you at the resort, where the signature Aman ritual of quiet, unhurried arrival begins. Change into sarongs and walk the 100 steps down to Pansea Beach — a private crescent of white sand with no vendors, no jet skis, and no distractions. Watch the Andaman sunset from the Terrace restaurant with a Singha in hand and let the week truly begin.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Aman Spa & Beach Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Aman Spa is renowned for its Thai therapeutic treatments, particularly the Aman Signature Thai Massage — a full two-hour traditional massage in an open-air pavilion surrounded by tropical gardens. Book the couples' treatment suite for a simultaneous session. Afternoons at Amanpuri pass in a golden haze of sun, sea, and silence at the beach or your private pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Phang Nga Bay by Longtail
itinerary_7_nights.description: Charter one of Amanpuri's traditional longtail boats for a private exploration of Phang Nga Bay — the extraordinary seascape of limestone karst towers rising from jade-green water made famous by the James Bond film 'The Man with the Golden Gun.' Pack a picnic prepared by the resort kitchen and anchor in a sea cave for a private lunch. Return by sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cooking Class & Yoga
itinerary_7_nights.description: Join the resort's Thai cooking class in the outdoor kitchen pavilion — learning to make tom kha gai, green curry, and mango sticky rice together is one of the most memorable honeymoon activities in Thailand. Balance the morning with an afternoon yoga session on the clifftop pavilion overlooking the Andaman Sea, taught by the resident Aman yoga teacher.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Island Hopping — Similan or Phi Phi
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Aman team can arrange a private speedboat excursion to the Phi Phi Islands or, for snorkeling enthusiasts willing to make the longer journey, the Similan Islands Marine National Park — among the top dive sites in Asia. Swim through pristine coral gardens with virtually no other boats in sight. This is the Thailand of postcards and dreams.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunset Yacht Charter
itinerary_7_nights.description: Charter the Aman's private sailing yacht for a half-day sunset cruise around the headlands of Phuket's west coast — stopping at hidden bays, drifting past sea cliffs, and dropping anchor for a swim in a turquoise cove while champagne is chilled. Return to the resort for a private beach barbecue dinner arranged by the butler, with Thai lanterns and fresh seafood grilled tableside.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Wake at dawn and walk the now-familiar steps to Pansea Beach one last time in the early morning light — the beach is yours entirely at this hour, with only the sound of the Andaman lapping the shore. A final traditional Thai breakfast of rice congee, fresh papaya, and lemongrass tea on your villa's terrace, then a leisurely departure orchestrated by your Aman butler.

caveats: Amanpuri is among the most expensive hotels in Southeast Asia — at $1,200–$4,000 per night, a 7-night stay represents a very significant investment; budget for all meals and activities as well, as these are priced at Aman levels.

The resort is deliberately small with limited facilities compared to large resort complexes — there is one beach, two restaurants, and one spa; couples seeking a variety of nightlife and off-resort dining will find the surrounding Surin Beach area quiet.
Phuket's southwest monsoon runs from May through October, bringing heavy rain and rough seas — the best months for Amanpuri are November through April when the Andaman coast is at its most beautiful.

faqs.question: Is Amanpuri good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: It is widely regarded as the finest honeymoon resort in Thailand and one of the best in the world. The combination of Pansea Beach's extraordinary natural setting, the Aman's unparalleled personalized service, private pool pavilions, and a spa rooted in authentic Thai healing traditions creates an experience that couples describe as the most romantic of their lives. The resort virtually defines the modern honeymoon experience in Asia.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: November through April is the dry season for Phuket's Andaman coast, with calm seas, low humidity, and consistent sunshine. January and February are peak months with the best snorkeling and diving conditions. May through October is monsoon season — the resort remains open and offers dramatic skies and lush green landscape, but seas can be rough and water activities are limited.

faqs.question: Are the private pool villas worth the upgrade?
faqs.answer: For honeymooners, absolutely. The private pool villas represent the full Aman experience — your own infinity pool overlooking the Andaman, a dedicated butler who anticipates your every need, and complete seclusion from other guests. Many couples who book a pavilion on arrival immediately request a villa upgrade after experiencing the property. If budget allows, start there.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Book your villa or pavilion as early as possible — Amanpuri has only 35 pavilions and 40 villas, and the best oceanview rooms are reserved months ahead. Spa treatments, boat excursions, and cooking class slots should all be arranged before arrival through the resort's pre-arrival planning team. Private yacht charters should be requested with at least 2 weeks' notice.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, Amanpuri welcomes all ages, including families. However, the resort's intimate scale, meditative atmosphere, and very high price point mean it attracts almost exclusively couples and mature travelers. The experience is essentially as private as an adults-only resort in practice.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Phuket International Airport (HKT). The resort arranges a private car transfer (approximately 35 minutes) or a dramatic helicopter transfer for an extra fee. Many couples traveling from Europe or the Americas connect through Bangkok (Suvarnabhumi Airport) on Thai Airways, Singapore Airlines, or a Middle Eastern carrier. From the UK, the flight is approximately 13–14 hours with one stop.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Amanpuri Phuket

Dear Amanpuri team,

My partner and I are planning our honeymoon and are very interested in staying with you from [DATE] to [DATE] ([X] nights).

We are particularly interested in: a Pool Pavilion or one-bedroom Pool Villa with Andaman Sea views.

Could you please let us know:
- Current availability for our dates
- Any honeymoon enhancements or complimentary arrangements you can offer
- Whether a private Phang Nga Bay longtail excursion and Thai cooking class can be arranged during our stay

Thank you for your time.

[Your names]
